title: Fixes bug whereby read_only bool is called as a function
body:
This is a simple bug fix to remedy the following issue:

```python
  File ".../site-packages/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py", line 374, in emacs_insert_mode
    or app.current_buffer.read_only()
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Exception 'bool' object is not callable
```

It would appear to be a simple typo, calling the boolean `read_only` property as if it were a function.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -371,7 +371,7 @@ def emacs_insert_mode() -> bool:
     if (
         app.editing_mode != EditingMode.EMACS
         or app.current_buffer.selection_state
-        or app.current_buffer.read_only()
+        or app.current_buffer.read_only
     ):
         return False
     return True

steps.operation: Fix incorrect invocation of the `read_only` boolean attribute by removing the call parentheses in the `emacs_insert_mode` filter logic.
steps.reason: `app.current_buffer.read_only` is a boolean property/attribute, but it was mistakenly called like a function (`read_only()`), causing a runtime `TypeError: 'bool' object is not callable` when evaluating the filter.
steps.side_effects: Changes the truthiness check in `emacs_insert_mode` from attempting a call (which previously raised) to a direct boolean evaluation; any code paths depending on this filter (e.g., Emacs insert-mode keybindings/behavior gating) will now function correctly and may alter behavior only insofar as the crash no longer occurs.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a runtime TypeError in the `emacs_insert_mode` filter by treating `current_buffer.read_only` as a boolean attribute rather than calling it as a function.
